title: Message Envelopes, Bytes, and Schema Evolution · Language Lens: Runtime Schema Validation
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must bound bytes before decoding, validate content metadata, preserve unknown-field policy, and test old/new schema cases; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the runtime schema validation increment in a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix. Learn…
objectives: Validate unknown envelopes and payloads after bounded decoding with required fields, semantic checks, collection limits, and explicit rejection evidence.

Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Runtime Schema Validation · Theory Runtime Schema Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 7430 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Runtime Schema Validation contract for the supplied data.
